questions.question: Why do consumers have to make choices when spending their income? A Advertising encourages consumer spending., B Consumers have unlimited incomes., C Consumer wants cannot all be satisfied with their income., D Not all products will be attractive to consumers.
questions.text: Why do consumers have to make choices when spending their income? A Advertising encourages consumer spending., B Consumers have unlimited incomes., C Consumer wants cannot all be satisfied with their income., D Not all products will be attractive to consumers.
options: C Consumer wants cannot all be satisfied with their income.
D Not all products will be attractive to consumers.

questions.question: Economics is divided into two: microeconomics and macroeconomics. statement 1  When the price of oil falls there is an expansion in demand. statement 2  The  Organization  of  the  Petroleum  Exporting  Countries  (OPEC)  can influence the price of oil. Which combination correctly describes these two statements? statement 1 statement 2 A macroeconomic statement    macroeconomic statement, B macroeconomic statement     microeconomic statement, C microeconomic statement     macroeconomic statement, D microeconomic statement     microeconomic statement

questions.question: What is a disadvantage of a market economic system? A Entrepreneurs lack incentives to produce what consumers want., B Government intervention will reduce the efficiency of firms., C Market dominance by firms can lead to higher prices., D There is underproduction of demerit goods.
questions.text: What is a disadvantage of a market economic system? A Entrepreneurs lack incentives to produce what consumers want., B Government intervention will reduce the efficiency of firms., C Market dominance by firms can lead to higher prices., D There is underproduction of demerit goods.
options: A Entrepreneurs lack incentives to produce what consumers want.
B Government intervention will reduce the efficiency of firms.
C Market dominance by firms can lead to higher prices.
D There is underproduction of demerit goods.

questions.question: The diagram shows the demand curve for coffee in the US. price quantity O demand for coffee P 1 Q 1 Q 2 P 2 Why did the quantity demanded move from Q 1 to Q 2 ? A Coffee became cheaper., B Incomes rose in the US., C There was a successful advertising campaign by coffee retailers., D The price of substitutes for coffee rose. 4
questions.text: The diagram shows the demand curve for coffee in the US. price quantity O demand for coffee P 1 Q 1 Q 2 P 2 Why did the quantity demanded move from Q 1 to Q 2 ? options: B Incomes rose in the US.
C There was a successful advertising campaign by coffee retailers.
D The price of substitutes for coffee rose. 4

questions.question: In an African country with large areas of tropical desert the price elasticity of demand for salt is highly inelastic. This will result in greater consumer expenditure on salt when price changes from P 1 to P 2 . Which diagram illustrates this situation? price of salt O quantity A D P 2 P 1 price of salt O quantity B D P 2 P 1 price of salt O quantity C D P 2 P 1 price of salt O quantity D D P 2 P 1

questions.question: In which occupation would wages tend to increase? A in those where a worker is paid weekly, B in those where a worker needs less training, C in those where there is an excess demand for labour, D in those where work becomes less dangerous
questions.text: In which occupation would wages tend to increase? A in those where a worker is paid weekly, B in those where a worker needs less training, C in those where there is an excess demand for labour, D in those where work becomes less dangerous
options: A in those where a worker is paid weekly
B in those where a worker needs less training
C in those where there is an excess demand for labour
D in those where work becomes less dangerous

questions.text: Which row shows a characteristic of money and a function of money? characteristic function A acceptability medium of exchange, B cash measure of value, C divisibility portability, D store of value bank deposit 5
options: D store of value bank deposit 5

The table shows the variable cost at different levels of output. output (units) 1 2 3 4 variable cost ($)    100    190    270    350 If the goods are sold for $500 each, how much profit will be made from the sale of 4 units? A $650 B  $1000 C  $1650 D  $2000 6

questions.question: Which combination is usually found in a monopoly? A many buyers and many sellers, B many buyers and single seller, C single buyer and many sellers, D single buyer and single seller
questions.text: Which combination is usually found in a monopoly? A many buyers and many sellers, B many buyers and single seller, C single buyer and many sellers, D single buyer and single seller
options: A many buyers and many sellers
B many buyers and single seller
C single buyer and many sellers
D single buyer and single seller

questions.question: What is usually considered to be an advantage to a firm of using division of labour? A high labour turnover, B increased mechanisation, C movement of labour between tasks, D need for quality control
questions.text: What is usually considered to be an advantage to a firm of using division of labour? A high labour turnover, B increased mechanisation, C movement of labour between tasks, D need for quality control
options: C movement of labour between tasks

questions.question: What are two aims that a government normally pursues when managing the performance of the whole economy? A to encourage an increase in production and to prevent high inflation, B to ensure no one is unemployed and to reduce exports, C to give everyone equal incomes and to increase government revenue, D to protect the environment and to reduce interest rates
questions.text: What are two aims that a government normally pursues when managing the performance of the whole economy? A to encourage an increase in production and to prevent high inflation, B to ensure no one is unemployed and to reduce exports, C to give everyone equal incomes and to increase government revenue, D to protect the environment and to reduce interest rates
options: A to encourage an increase in production and to prevent high inflation
B to ensure no one is unemployed and to reduce exports
C to give everyone equal incomes and to increase government revenue
D to protect the environment and to reduce interest rates

questions.question: What is most likely to be the responsibility of a central government? A the provision of immigration officials at an airport, B the provision of security cameras in a shopping centre, C the provision of security staff at a bank, D the provision of ticket inspectors on a train
questions.text: What is most likely to be the responsibility of a central government? A the provision of immigration officials at an airport, B the provision of security cameras in a shopping centre, C the provision of security staff at a bank, D the provision of ticket inspectors on a train
options: A the provision of immigration officials at an airport
B the provision of security cameras in a shopping centre
C the provision of security staff at a bank
D the provision of ticket inspectors on a train

questions.question: A government decreased the tax on all goods produced in its country. What is the most likely consequence of this? A a decrease in exports, B an increase in imports, C an increase in production, D a worsening of the balance of trade in goods 7
questions.text: A government decreased the tax on all goods produced in its country. What is the most likely consequence of this? A a decrease in exports, B an increase in imports, C an increase in production, D a worsening of the balance of trade in goods 7
options: D a worsening of the balance of trade in goods 7

questions.question: Which government policy measure would cause an increase in a country’s supply of labour? A increasing unemployment benefits, B lowering interest rates, C raising income tax, D raising the age of retirement
questions.text: Which government policy measure would cause an increase in a country’s supply of labour? A increasing unemployment benefits, B lowering interest rates, C raising income tax, D raising the age of retirement
options: D raising the age of retirement

questions.question: An economy is experiencing rising prices. Which government policy will help reduce consumer expenditure? A introducing compulsory saving for income earners, B investing more in building infrastructure, C issuing more banknotes and coins, D reducing indirect taxes
questions.text: An economy is experiencing rising prices. Which government policy will help reduce consumer expenditure? A introducing compulsory saving for income earners, B investing more in building infrastructure, C issuing more banknotes and coins, D reducing indirect taxes
options: A introducing compulsory saving for income earners
B investing more in building infrastructure
C issuing more banknotes and coins

questions.question: Some goods take a greater percentage of a typical household’s total spending than others. How is this accounted for in the construction of a consumer prices index? A by deducting the goods, B by giving a weight to the goods, C by taking an average of price fluctuations during a year, D by using the price elasticity of demand for the goods

options: B by giving a weight to the goods
C by taking an average of price fluctuations during a year
D by using the price elasticity of demand for the goods

questions.question: Which factor in the world’s low-income countries limits their economic development? A balance of payments surpluses, B high saving ratios, C large inflows of foreign investment, D rapid population growth 9
questions.text: Which factor in the world’s low-income countries limits their economic development? A balance of payments surpluses, B high saving ratios, C large inflows of foreign investment, D rapid population growth 9
options: C large inflows of foreign investment
